name: Port Scan Detection & Response
description: Detects and responds to port scanning activity targeting internal or external-facing assets. Riggs AI enriches the source IP across threat intelligence feeds and determines scan intent (reconnaissance, vulnerability probing, or worm propagation). External malicious scanners are blocked on the firewall and added to the EDL blocklist. Internal scan sources trigger a lateral movement investigation. Low-risk scans from known vulnerability scanners are whitelisted and closed.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: VirusTotal
required_integrations.reason: IP reputation lookup for the scanning source

required_integrations.name: AbuseIPDB
required_integrations.reason: Check abuse reports and scan history for the source IP

required_integrations.name: Palo Alto NGFW
required_integrations.reason: Block confirmed malicious external scanners at the perimeter

config.description: Triggers when IDS/IPS or network monitoring detects port scanning activity against internal or external assets

config.additional_instructions: Enrich the source IP across VirusTotal, AbuseIPDB, and GreyNoise. Determine if this is a known scanner, an authorized vulnerability assessment tool, or a malicious reconnaissance actor. Check if source is internal or external.
config.description: AI-powered enrichment of the scanning source IP to determine reputation, origin (internal vs external), and likely intent
Routes based on whether the scanner is an external malicious source versus an internal or authorized scanner

parameters.comment: Port scanner blocked — Scanned {{trigger_1.alert.ports_scanned}} ports, Verdict: {{analyze_1.verdict}}

config.description: Blocks the external malicious scanner IP on the perimeter firewall to prevent further reconnaissance

parameters.comment: Malicious port scanner — {{trigger_1.alert.ports_scanned}} ports scanned against {{trigger_1.alert.target_ip}}

config.description: Adds the scanner IP to the EDL blocklist for organization-wide perimeter blocking
Notifies the SOC team that an external port scanner has been blocked

parameters.note: Port scan from {{trigger_1.alert.source_ip}} analyzed — Verdict: {{analyze_1.verdict}}. Source identified as {{analyze_1.source_type}}. No block action required.

config.description: Annotates the alert when the scanner is internal or an authorized vulnerability assessment tool and closes without action
Port scan detection and response workflow complete
